scripts.name: AWS CLI: basic public S3 bucket exposure check
scripts.body:
# Requires configured AWS CLI with permissions to list and read S3 ACLs
aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[].Name" --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read B; do
  echo "Bucket: $B"
  aws s3api get-bucket-acl --bucket "$B" --query "Grants[].Grantee.URI" --output text 2>/dev/null |
    egrep "AllUsers|AuthenticatedUsers" && echo "  [!] Bucket may be publicly accessible"
done

scripts.name: M365: search for suspicious sign‑ins in unified audit log
scripts.body:
# Requires Exchange Online / Security & Compliance modules and permissions to read audit logs
Search-UnifiedAuditLog -StartDate (Get-Date).AddDays(-3) -EndDate (Get-Date) -Operations UserLoggedIn |
  Where-Object { $_.ClientIP -notlike "YOUR_TRUSTED_RANGE*" } |
  Select-Object UserId, ClientIP, Operation, CreationDate

done

scripts.name: AWS CLI: basic public S3 bucket exposure check
scripts.body:
# Requires configured AWS CLI with permissions to list and read S3 ACLs
aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[].Name" --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read B; do
  echo "Bucket: $B"
  aws s3api get-bucket-acl --bucket "$B" --query "Grants[].Grantee.URI" --output text 2>/dev/null |
    egrep "AllUsers|AuthenticatedUsers" && echo "  [!] Bucket may be publicly accessible"
done

recommendations: Run targeted awareness for users most likely to be impacted by the described phishing templates.
Review MFA policies and disable legacy authentication protocols (POP/IMAP/SMTP basic auth and other non‑MFA flows).

scripts.name: M365: search for suspicious sign‑ins in unified audit log
scripts.body:
# Requires Exchange Online / Security & Compliance modules and permissions to read audit logs
Search-UnifiedAuditLog -StartDate (Get-Date).AddDays(-3) -EndDate (Get-Date) -Operations UserLoggedIn |
  Where-Object { $_.ClientIP -notlike "YOUR_TRUSTED_RANGE*" } |
  Select-Object UserId, ClientIP, Operation, CreationDate

items.title: NIST to stop rating non-priority flaws due to volume increase
items.summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology will stop assigning severity scores to lower-priority vulnerabilities due to the growing workload from rising submission volumes. [...]
recommendations: Run an out-of-band malware scan on servers and endpoints, focusing on recent changes.
Collect suspicious binaries from Temp/AppData and submit them to a sandbox or reverse engineering pipeline.

scripts.name: PowerShell: hunt for suspicious processes and autoruns
scripts.body:
Get-WmiObject Win32_Process |
  Where-Object { $_.Path -and ($_.Path -like "*AppData*" -or $_.Path -like "*Temp*") } |
  Select-Object ProcessId, Name, Path

Get-CimInstance Win32_StartupCommand |
  Select-Object Name, Command, Location

scripts.name: Linux: hunt for suspicious processes and SUID binaries
scripts.body:
ps aux | egrep "crypto|minerd|xmrig|kdevtmpfsi" | grep -v egrep || echo "No obvious miners found"

find / -xdev -type f -perm -4000 2>/dev/null

scripts.name: AWS CLI: basic public S3 bucket exposure check
scripts.body:
# Requires configured AWS CLI with permissions to list and read S3 ACLs
aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[].Name" --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read B; do
  echo "Bucket: $B"
  aws s3api get-bucket-acl --bucket "$B" --query "Grants[].Grantee.URI" --output text 2>/dev/null |
    egrep "AllUsers|AuthenticatedUsers" && echo "  [!] Bucket may be publicly accessible"
done

items.title: Critical flaw in Protobuf library enables JavaScript code execution
items.summary: Proof-of-concept exploit code has been published for a critical remote code execution flaw in protobuf.js, a widely used JavaScript implementation of Google's Protocol Buffers. [...]
recommendations: Map affected product versions from the article to the software actually deployed in your environment.
If no vendor patch is available, implement temporary mitigations such as WAF rules, strict access control, and additional segmentation.

scripts.name: Search for requests related to this article URL in web logs (Linux)
scripts.body:
# Replace access.log paths with your actual web server logs
grep -i "https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/critical-flaw-in-protobuf-library-enables-javascript-code-execution/" /var/log/nginx/access.log* /var/log/apache2/access.log* 2>/dev/null || echo "No hits for indicator"

items.title: Microsoft Teams right-click paste broken by Edge update bug
items.summary: Microsoft is warning that a recent Microsoft Edge browser update introduced a bug that breaks right-click paste in chats in the Microsoft Teams desktop client. [...]
recommendations: Assess the relevance of this story to your environment and map the described techniques to your technology stack.

scripts.name: Generic: search for IOCs from the article across logs
scripts.body:
# Replace PATTERN with domains/IPs/URLs or other indicators extracted from the article:
grep -Ei "PATTERN" /var/log/* 2>/dev/null || echo "No hits for pattern"

scripts.name: ESXi / Linux: review SSH authentication attempts
scripts.body:

grep -i "sshd" /var/log/auth.log /var/log/messages* 2>/dev/null | egrep "Failed|Accepted"
